name: Hallucination Detector
description: Compare generated content claims against original source material to flag statements that aren't supported by any source.
usage_notes: Run after content-writer has produced content_markdown and page-scraper/browser-scraper has produced text_content. Uses LLM to verify each factual claim against source material. If no source text_content is available, the entity passes with a warning (cannot verify without sources).

options.name: pass_threshold
options.label: Pass threshold
options.description: Minimum hallucination_score (0-1) for qa_pass to be true. 1.0 means every claim must be verified.

options.name: ai_model
options.label: AI Model
options.description: Which model to use for claim verification.

options.name: max_source_chars
options.label: Max source characters
options.description: Maximum total characters of source text to include in LLM context. Truncates oldest sources first if exceeded.

options.name: claims_per_batch
options.label: Claims per batch
options.description: Number of claims to verify in each LLM call. Higher values use fewer API calls but risk hitting context limits.

options.name: prompt
options.label: Verification prompt
options.description: System prompt for the LLM claim verification. Use {{CLAIMS}} and {{SOURCES}} as placeholders.
options.default:
You are a fact-checking assistant. You will be given a list of factual claims extracted from a generated article, and the original source material the article was based on.

For each claim, determine whether it is supported by the source material.

Rules:
- "supported" = the source material contains information that directly or clearly supports this claim, even if paraphrased
- "unsupported" = the source material does NOT contain information supporting this claim, and it is NOT general common knowledge
- "partial" = the source material partially supports the claim but key details (numbers, dates, specifics) differ or are missing
- General knowledge claims (e.g. "Malta is a popular iGaming jurisdiction", "the iGaming industry is growing") should be marked "supported" even if not explicitly in sources
- If the claim references data from an analysis or summary derived from the sources, mark it "supported"

Return a JSON array (no markdown fences, no extra text) with one object per claim:
[
  {
    "claim": "the exact claim text",
    "verdict": "supported" | "unsupported" | "partial",
    "quote": "the supporting quote from sources, or null if unsupported",
    "severity": "low" | "medium" | "high"
  }
]

Severity guide:
- "low" = general phrasing, opinion, or common knowledge that is hard to verify
- "medium" = specific factual claim (company name, product, feature) not found in sources
- "high" = specific number, date, statistic, or financial claim not found in sources

CLAIMS:
{{CLAIMS}}

SOURCE MATERIAL:
{{SOURCES}}
